description: The Worker-communist Party of Iran (WPI) was founded in 1991 by [Mansoor Hekmat](/people/mansoor-hekmat) and other members who split from the [Communist Party of Iran](/orgs/communist-party-of-iran). The party rejects both the Soviet model and Third World nationalism, advocating instead for a form of Marxism centred on working-class self-emancipation and secularism. Its primary slogans are 'Liberty, Equality, Workers' Rule' and 'For a Socialist Republic.' Hekmat led the party until his death in 2002, after which a leadership dispute split the WPI into two factions: one under Hamid Taqvaee and another under Koorosh Modarresi, who founded a separate Worker-communist Party (Hekmatist). The WPI has been active in international solidarity campaigns, particularly around women's rights, and opposed the Islamic Republic from exile.
editorial_notes: Founded by Mansoor Hekmat. Split after his death in 2002. Active in exile.
